reviews.review_msg: I enjoyed working in my group (for once, everyone carried themselves) but the content felt really dry and i don't think it really set me up for a job (unlike what we were told in class). I heard from my friends that the 276 was their highlight course but ig their prof was different. i'd avoid kasra if you can

chill guy but oh man was the course disorganized... I love Kasra but I don't think he's cut out to be a prof. Get ready to do most of the learning on your own and carry team members in the project. We didn't have assignment grades until the very end of the semester.

He's chill but a little disorganized. He'd lose sight of macro concepts mid-lecture and assignments weren't graded until the end of the semester. Lectures were mostly not relevant for the group project, so get ready to teach yourself A LOT. Also pray you get assigned good teammates — I lost a lot of marks from things outside of my control

This prof is the nicest person/prof that you could meet at SFU. He is very helpful, caring, approachable, and whatnot (man, I wish this guy the best). The only thing I could complain about is that the course is pretty vague; you have to learn most framework by yourself. Get yourself to self-learn about Docker, Alembic, api, deployment, CICD, etc.

Great chill W prof. Just focus on the team project and know the basics of class content from a general perspctive and u also good to go for quizzes as well. Even gives a lesson after final grades released lol.

Kasra is a great prof for 276 since he actively works as a software engineer. He is able to give insight into what its like working as a software engineer currently because of this. The class is mostly just a big group project, and the difficulty will depend on the group members you get (random). His lectures are useful and not dry.

Kasra himself is a very chill, friendly and approachable guy. However I feel like he is not ready to teach cmpt 276 yet due to the lack of structure in the course. Frankly, I felt like learning nothing after a semester (or dont get taught to know where to apply what I have learned). I got stressed out so much after taking this course with him.

Kasra is the worst prof for 276. He doesn't have enough skill to teach in 276. His lecture material is so random. As an introductory software development course, students didn't understand what he was saying. Lack of experience, the course was so tough, although 276 is one of the easiest courses. It was my biggest mistake of year to take this.
